title: Sarcomphalus mauritianus

intro: Sarcomphalus mauritianus, also known as Mauritius' Sarcomphalus, is a shrub native to the tropical forests of Madagascar, belonging to the family Rhamnaceae. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its reported anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antipyretic properties. Bioactive constituents of Sarcomphalus mauritianus include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being incorporated into topical creams and ointments for its anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving effects.
